assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md contains '## Classification: code change request' and 'Sub-task will be created to address this feedback.' The file subtask-30001.md exists. report.md confirms: '30001 | reviewer-a | code change request | Yes -- transaction wrapping for soft_delete method'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: suggestion'. The Reasoning section explicitly analyzes the suggestive language: '"should also" -- suggestive; "also" implies an optional enhancement beyond the current scope', '"would help" -- conditional language, not imperative', '"Something like:" -- hedging language presenting an example approach, not a prescribed fix'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md contains '## Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task created. Nits are informational feedback that do not require tracked work.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md contains '## Classification: question' and 'No sub-task created. Questions ask for clarification and do not require code changes.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, value 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 19).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 29) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 30).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 32) with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File reference, and the full original review comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and the report (report.md). All are markdown documentation/analysis files. No source code files (.rs, .sql, .toml, etc.) were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md). The report.md Review Feedback Classification table lists all 4 comments with their classifications: 30001 (code change request), 30002 (suggestion), 30003 (nit), 30004 (question).

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md overall status is 'WARN' with no mention of merging. The report only creates classifications and sub-tasks. No merge action is indicated anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file); no existing tests modified or deleted |'. The detailed section (lines 101-102) confirms: 'Only a new test file was added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs). No existing test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '## Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section (lines 29-47) with subsections 'Evaluation' (checking CONVENTIONS.md, documented conventions, codebase patterns) and 'Upgrade Decision' ('The suggestion is not upgraded. No documented project convention backs the requirement.'). report.md also contains a 'Convention Upgrade Evaluation (Comment 30002)' section (lines 71-79) with the same analysis.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'No sub-task created. Suggestions are informational feedback that do not require tracked work unless backed by a project convention that upgrades them.' No subtask-30002.md file exists. report.md confirms: '30002 | reviewer-a | suggestion | No -- index recommendation; convention upgrade evaluated, no backing convention found'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 97-98: 'Eval Quality -- N/A  3-criteria detection found no eval result reviews in the PR. No reviews match the detection heuristic (author `github-actions[bot]`, marker `## Eval Results`, footer `sdlc-workflow/run-evals`).' Also line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR'. Test Quality overall is PASS, not affected by Eval Quality.
